stories.headline: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5, a Cheaper, More Agentic Model That Rivals Opus
stories.key_takeaway: Anthropic's Claude Sonnet 5 delivers near-flagship agentic performance at less than half the price of Opus 4.8, signaling that cheap, reliable autonomy is now the industry's competitive frontier.

pull_quote.text: We handed Claude Sonnet 5 a two-part job - update Salesforce account tiers, send a launch announcement to enterprise contacts - and it finished end to end. That used to stall halfway. For day-to-day automation, it's a no-brainer.

data_points.label: Intro price per M input/output tokens

stories.article_excerpt: Anthropic is betting that the next race in AI will be won not by the model that thinks hardest, but by the one that works cheapest.

stories.headline: Meituan Open-Sources LongCat-2.0, a 1.6-Trillion-Parameter Model Trained Entirely on Chinese Chips
stories.key_takeaway: Meituan says LongCat-2.0 is the first trillion-parameter model to complete both pre-training and inference entirely on domestic Chinese chips, weakening a core assumption behind US export controls on advanced AI hardware.

pull_quote.text: LongCat-2.0 was the industry's first trillion-parameter model to complete full-process training and inference on a 50,000-card domestic computing power cluster.

stories.article_excerpt: A company most of the world files under food delivery just moved the goalposts in the U.S.-China chip war with a 1.6-trillion-parameter open model.

stories.headline: Google's AI Coding Strike Team Pivots to Midtraining After Losing Six Researchers in Five Months
stories.key_takeaway: Google DeepMind has restructured its emergency AI Coding Strike Team to add a dedicated midtraining phase built on its proprietary codebase, even as six named researchers defected to Meta, OpenAI, and Anthropic since February.

pull_quote.text: To win the final sprint, we must urgently bridge the gap in agentic execution and turn our models into primary developers of final code.
pull_quote.role: Google co-founder, in an internal memo

stories.article_excerpt: Ten weeks after Sergey Brin scrambled an emergency strike team to rescue Google from Anthropic's lead in AI coding, the effort has been redrawn.

stories.headline: GitHub Copilot's First Metered Billing Cycle Closes With Bills 10x to 50x Higher for Power Users
stories.key_takeaway: The close of GitHub Copilot's first usage-based billing cycle on June 30 confirmed 10x to 50x cost surges for agentic power users, marking the definitive end of flat-rate AI coding as the industry converges on metered pricing.

pull_quote.text: This new usage model is just stupidly expensive. I'm adjusting mine by cancelling. At that cost, it is no longer cost-effective or useful in any practical way.
pull_quote.role: Copilot user whose bill was set to rise from ~$29 to nearly $750/month

data_points.label: Cost of a single agentic session

stories.article_excerpt: GitHub promised a fairer system that would charge developers for what they consumed. On June 30, the first full cycle closed and the invoices landed.

stories.headline: Google Opens Nano Banana Personalized Image Generation to Free Gemini Users in the US
stories.key_takeaway: Google made Gemini's Nano Banana-powered personalized image generation free for eligible US users, a strategic play to grow adoption of its 750M-user Gemini app.

pull_quote.text: Starting today, all eligible users in the U.S. can experience deeply personalized image generation in Gemini for free. We're connecting Personal Intelligence with Nano Banana and Google Photos, so your creations can easily reflect your taste and lifestyle.

stories.article_excerpt: Google is betting the fastest way to hook casual users on generative AI is to let them turn themselves and their pets into custom artwork for free.

stories.headline: Alphabet Closes $84.75 Billion Equity Raise, the Largest AI Infrastructure Financing in Corporate History
stories.key_takeaway: Alphabet upsized its equity raise from $80B to $84.75B after heavy oversubscription, the largest such financing in corporate history, to fund an AI compute buildout that Sundar Pichai says still cannot keep pace with demand.

pull_quote.text: The logic is simple: under-investing is an existential risk; over-investing is merely expensive.
pull_quote.role: Co-head of equity capital markets for the Americas, Mergermarket

stories.article_excerpt: Alphabet went to market asking for $80 billion on a Monday. By Tuesday, investors had handed it $84.75 billion and asked to give more.

stories.headline: Berkshire Hathaway Anchors Alphabet's AI Raise With a $10 Billion Private Placement
stories.key_takeaway: Berkshire Hathaway's $10 billion private placement anchoring Alphabet's record raise is a classic value-investing move - buying a cash-generating franchise at a sentiment-driven discount after talent departures wiped $269 billion off the market cap.

pull_quote.text: When it goes down, we love it, because we'll buy more. And if it goes up, it kills us to buy more.

data_points.label: Market cap wiped in June selloff

stories.article_excerpt: When Alphabet needed an anchor for the largest equity raise in tech history, it turned to the most famously patient buyer in American finance.

stories.headline: Amazon's Custom Silicon Hits a $20 Billion Annual Run Rate as AI Chip Diversification Turns Real
stories.key_takeaway: Amazon's in-house chip business has crossed a $20B annual run rate growing over 100% year over year, and multi-year Trainium commitments from Anthropic, OpenAI, Meta and Uber signal that efforts to reduce Nvidia dependence are producing real revenue.

pull_quote.text: If our chips business was a standalone business and sold chips produced this year to AWS and other third parties as other leading chip companies do, our annual revenue run rate would be $50 billion.

stories.article_excerpt: Every hyperscaler talked about building its own chips, then kept writing checks to Nvidia. Amazon just put a number on the alternative.

stories.headline: BMW i Ventures Launches a $300 Million Fund Targeting Agentic and Physical AI Startups
stories.key_takeaway: BMW i Ventures launched a $300M third fund for seed-through-Series B startups in agentic AI, physical AI, industrial software and supply chain tech, lifting its capital under management to $1.1B and signaling that Tier 1 industrial firms are using corporate VC to secure early access to factory-grade AI.

pull_quote.text: AI is quickly becoming part of the operating system of the modern enterprise. We're particularly interested in AI that can execute workflows end-to-end, driving real ROI through autonomous systems.

stories.article_excerpt: BMW's venture arm has decided the future of the automobile will be decided as much on the factory floor as on the road.

stories.headline: Pocket Raises $11 Million as AI Note-Taking Hardware Finds a Real Market
stories.key_takeaway: Pocket's $11M raise, backed by 130,000+ units sold and $27M ARR, signals that AI hardware works when it solves one narrow, practical workflow rather than trying to replace the phone - a sharp contrast to failed gadgets like the Humane AI Pin and Rabbit R1.

pull_quote.text: We thought every meeting notetaker was built for online conversations, but nothing was geared towards real-life talk. AI really needs a lot of context to work better for us, and a lot of that context exists offline.

stories.article_excerpt: The graveyard of AI hardware is littered with gadgets that promised to replace the phone. Pocket's difference this time is traction.

stories.headline: RAISE US Launches With $500 Million to Retrain American Workers for the AI Economy
stories.key_takeaway: Gina Raimondo and Eric Holcomb launched RAISE US, a bipartisan nonprofit backed by $500M+ from the very AI companies driving job disruption, to test employer-linked worker retraining in four states despite a federal track record showing such programs rarely move workers to safer jobs.

pull_quote.text: America has a technology strategy for leading the global AI competition. It does not yet have a people strategy, and we cannot lead without one.
pull_quote.role: CEO of RAISE US and former U.S. Commerce Secretary

data_points.label: Workers expecting AI to do most of their job within a year
US job cuts tied to AI in 2026

stories.article_excerpt: America has a plan to win the race to build AI. It does not yet have a plan for the people the technology leaves behind.

stories.headline: Meta Hired Hundreds of Contractors to Pose as Minors and Probe Rival AI Chatbots
stories.key_takeaway: Meta secretly ran a project in which hundreds of contractors posed as minors to bombard rival chatbots from OpenAI, Google, and Character.AI with tens of thousands of crisis-styled prompts, raising sharp ethical and regulatory questions about covert competitive safety benchmarking.

pull_quote.text: Testing and benchmarking chatbot responses to help ensure safe and age-appropriate experiences is a responsible, industry-standard practice.

data_points.label: Prompts sent to rival chatbots in one round
Sex/romance prompts in one reviewed sheet
Children 9-17 who have used AI chatbots

stories.article_excerpt: Meta ran a covert operation in which contractors posed as children to stress-test the guardrails of its rivals' chatbots, a WIRED investigation found.

stories.headline: Taiwan Investigates Alleged Diversion of Super Micro AI Servers to China
stories.key_takeaway: Taiwan launched its first criminal crackdown on AI-hardware diversion, raiding Super Micro's local offices over roughly 50 Nvidia-equipped servers allegedly smuggled to China - a probe that tests whether export controls can be enforced at the assembled-system level.

pull_quote.text: Super Micro is working closely with Taiwanese authorities and remains committed to protecting its technology and intellectual property.
pull_quote.role: Company statement responding to the raids

data_points.label: Nvidia servers allegedly routed to China
Value in parallel U.S. DOJ indictment
Super Micro share drop on the news

stories.article_excerpt: Taiwanese prosecutors raided Super Micro's local offices in a widening probe into whether high-end AI servers were illegally diverted to China.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Limits Geofence Warrants in a Landmark Digital-Privacy Ruling
stories.key_takeaway: In a 6-3 ruling in Chatrie v. United States, the Supreme Court held that geofence warrants sweeping up location data on everyone near a crime scene are Fourth Amendment searches, extending Carpenter and forcing law enforcement to meet probable-cause and particularity requirements.

pull_quote.text: An individual has a reasonable expectation of privacy in records about his cell phone's location, and police intrude on that constitutionally protected interest when they demand the information - even though for only a limited time, and from a third-party tech company.
pull_quote.role: Writing for the 6-3 majority in Chatrie v. United States

data_points.label: Vote treating geofence warrants as searches
Radius of the Google location dump
Carpenter, the precedent it builds on

stories.article_excerpt: The Supreme Court ruled that compelling a tech company to sweep up location data from everyone near a crime scene is a Fourth Amendment search.

stories.headline: Researchers Unveil Neuromorphic Artificial Skin That Lets Robots Feel Touch and Pain in Real Time
stories.key_takeaway: City University of Hong Kong researchers built a neuromorphic robotic e-skin that encodes touch as nerve-like spikes, detects pain and injury, and triggers protective reflexes locally by bypassing the central processor - a step toward safer, more energy-efficient humanoid robots.

pull_quote.text: Our neuromorphic robotic e-skin features hierarchical, neural-inspired architecture enabling high-resolution touch sensing, active pain and injury detection with local reflexes, and modular quick-release repair.
pull_quote.attribution: Research team, City University of Hong Kong

data_points.label: Layers mimicking skin and sensory nerves
To swap a damaged skin patch

stories.article_excerpt: A team at City University of Hong Kong built an artificial skin that fires protective reflexes for robots without routing the emergency through a brain.
